_meta.note: cross-cutting schema-integrity fix; filed here as a home — affects evaluation_ops, notification_ops, results, reports, exam_material_ops

title: Fix systemic generated-DDL bug: single-column UNIQUE constraints that break one-to-many + versioning
business_reason: Migration 0022 fixed candidate_results, but the same generated-DDL bug exists across the schema: the generator added single-column UNIQUE constraints on FK and version columns that must NOT be globally unique. Each silently breaks a subsystem (one row per batch / one entity per version number): evaluation_candidate_responses (candidate_id, import_batch_id) and evaluation_candidate_scores (candidate_id, score_batch_id) make OMR import + scoring impossible (one candidate per batch); notification_recipients (batch_id) allows one recipient per batch; key_version/setting_version/definition_version/report_version/template_version unique GLOBALLY means only one entity can ever hold a given version number. These are correctness landmines (only unnoticed because those modules are scaffold). Replace each with the correct natural key.

fixes.evaluation_candidate_responses: drop uq(candidate_id) + uq(import_batch_id); add uq(import_batch_id, candidate_id)
fixes.evaluation_candidate_scores: drop uq(candidate_id) + uq(score_batch_id); add uq(score_batch_id, candidate_id)
fixes.evaluation_answer_keys: drop uq(key_version); add uq(exam_cycle_id, subject_code, grade_code, paper_set_code, key_version)
fixes.kept_intentional: staff_profiles.user_id, portal_roles.role_id/directus_role_id (one-to-one); exam_slot_bookings.participation_id left as-is (ambiguous — flagged for review); business *_code uniques are correct

param_14.question: Which generated single-column UNIQUE constraints are wrong, and what are the correct natural keys?
param_14.answer: FK columns (import_batch_id, score_batch_id, batch_id) and version columns (key_version, *_version) were made globally unique by the DDL generator, which forbids one-to-many rows and per-entity versioning. Each is replaced by the correct composite natural key (parent + discriminator). One-to-one uniques (staff_profiles.user_id, portal_roles) are kept.
param_14.answer_source: 0001_schema.sql unique constraints vs CANONICAL_DATA_MODEL.json columns/FKs; the candidate_results fix in 0022 established the pattern
param_14.security_implications: Data-integrity: the new composite keys still prevent true duplicates (same candidate twice in a batch, same version twice for one entity) while allowing the legitimate one-to-many. No data exposure.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: Unblocks OMR import + scoring (next P1), notification fan-out, and settings/report/material versioning. Adds a guardrail (check_unique_constraints.py) so the bad class can't regress.
param_14.assumptions: Affected tables are empty/scaffold (so adding composites won't fail on existing duplicates); supabase db push applies 0023.
needs_user_review: exam_slot_bookings.participation_id single-column unique — keep (one booking per participation) or relax for re-booking after cancel?
